decision_summary.rationale: Instance is idle and can be safely stopped.

i-b101.technical_explanation: The instance has been running with low CPU utilization (3.1% P95) for an extended period, indicating it is idle and can be stopped to save costs without impacting service availability.

cost_report.waste_evidence: P95 CPU (3.1%) below thresholds over 7 days, waste per month: $30.05

decision_summary.rationale: Instance is actively used and should remain running.

i-b102.technical_explanation: The instance is actively used with high CPU utilization (61.4% P95), indicating it should remain running to support the frontend service.

llm_response.group_summary: The frontend service instances have been evaluated. Instance i-b101 is idle and can be stopped to save costs, while instance i-b102 is actively used and should remain running. The total monthly savings from stopping i-b101 are $30.05, resulting in an annual savings of $360.6.

nl_quality.name: nl_quality
nl_quality.details: The report is well-structured and references specific metrics (CPU percentages, resource IDs) but the cost figures are internally inconsistent (current_monthly_cost $299.32 vs monthly_savings $30.05 implies ~10% savings, yet projected_monthly_cost is $0, suggesting a stop action should save the full $299.32, not $30.05), and the rationale lacks explanation of why alternatives were not chosen.
